title: Toilet cleaning powder
subtitle: A fizzing bicarbonate and citric acid powder with tea tree essential oil for toilet bowls
excerpt: A dry toilet cleaning powder made from bicarbonate of soda, citric acid, and tea tree essential oil. Sprinkle into the bowl, leave for 15 minutes while the fizzing loosens mineral deposits and kills surface bacteria, then scrub and flush. Makes enough for 6 to 8 cleaning sessions.
sourceNotes: Acid-base fizzing action for mineral scale removal: standard chemistry. Carson et al., Melaleuca alternifolia antimicrobial properties (Clinical Microbiology Reviews, 2006). Tea tree EO at 1-2% concentration as a surface antimicrobial.

content.text: A toilet cleaning powder works in two ways. The fizzing reaction of citric acid and bicarbonate dissolves
kills surface bacteria. The blend stands in for bleach-based cleaners in many homes. It is gentler than commercial bleach.

Keep the dry powder away from all moisture sources. Tea tree essential oil is toxic to cats; keep pets away from freshly cleaned toilets until the bowl has been flushed. Do not mix this product with bleach or commercial toilet cleaners.

items.name: Bicarbonate of soda
items.prepNote: Plain bicarbonate of soda. The base component of the acid-base pair; also provides mild abrasion.

items.name: Citric acid
items.prepNote: Fine citric acid granules. Dissolves limescale on contact with water. Do not inhale the dust.

items.name: Tea tree essential oil
items.prepNote: 2.3% of powder weight. Mix into the bicarbonate first; the dry powder absorbs and distributes the EO. Toxic to cats.

content.text: Weigh the bicarbonate of soda into the mixing bowl. Add the tea tree essential oil a few drops at a time, stirring continuously until the bicarbonate has absorbed it evenly with no visible oil puddles. Add the citric acid and stir briefly to combine. Transfer to a sealed glass jar and label with the making date.

Sprinkle 25 to 30 g of powder into the toilet bowl, concentrating it under the rim. The powder will fizz as it contacts the water. Leave for 15 minutes. Scrub the bowl with a toilet brush, paying attention to any staining under the rim and the water line. Flush. Use a dry spoon to scoop powder from the jar each time.

Store in a sealed glass jar away from the toilet's moisture environment; keep in a cupboard rather than on the cistern. The powder keeps for 12 months if kept dry. Any premature fizzing or clumping is a sign that moisture has entered the jar. Discard and remake a fresh batch.

recipe.yieldDescription: ~210 g powder (6 to 8 toilet cleaning sessions)
